cards.back: 虽然很难，但是我很开心。
cards.romanized: Suīrán hěn nán, dànshì wǒ hěn kāixīn.
cards.grammarNote: 虽然...但是... = although...but... Both halves are typically used together in Chinese, unlike English where you choose one. This is a concession pattern.

cards.front: Although tired, I feel very satisfied.
Although he's very busy, he always helps others.
cards.back: 虽然他很忙，但是他总是帮助别人。
cards.romanized: Suīrán tā hěn máng, dànshì tā zǒngshì bāngzhù biérén.

cards.front: Although the weather was bad, we still went out.
cards.back: 虽然天气不好，但是我们还是出去了。
cards.romanized: Suīrán tiānqì bù hǎo, dànshì wǒmen háishi chūqù le.

cards.front: Although I'm very sad, I understand.
cards.back: 虽然很伤心，但是我理解。
cards.romanized: Suīrán hěn shāngxīn, dànshì wǒ lǐjiě.

cards.front: Although the pressure was great, he didn't give up.
cards.back: 虽然压力很大，但是他没有放弃。
cards.romanized: Suīrán yālì hěn dà, dànshì tā méiyǒu fàngqì.

cards.front: I really want to go, but I'm a bit tired.
cards.romanized: Wǒ hěn xiǎng qù, búguò yǒudiǎn lèi.
cards.grammarNote: 不过 (búguò) = however / but — softer and more conversational than 但是. Common in casual speech.

cards.front: She said she was happy, but she looked upset.
cards.back: 她说很开心，可是看起来很难过。
cards.romanized: Tā shuō hěn kāixīn, kěshì kàn qǐlái hěn nánguò.
cards.grammarNote: 可是 (kěshì) = but — slightly softer than 但是. Both 可是 and 不过 are common alternatives in everyday speech.

cards.front: Although I was nervous, I did well.
cards.back: 虽然很紧张，但是我表现得很好。
cards.romanized: Suīrán hěn jǐnzhāng, dànshì wǒ biǎoxiàn de hěn hǎo.

cards.front: Although I was angry, I stayed calm.
cards.back: 虽然很生气，但是我保持了平静。
cards.romanized: Suīrán hěn shēngqì, dànshì wǒ bǎochí le píngjìng.

cards.front: Although I'm stressed, I'm still happy.
cards.back: 虽然有压力，但是我还是很开心。
cards.romanized: Suīrán yǒu yālì, dànshì wǒ háishi hěn kāixīn.

cards.front: Although she cried, she's fine now.
cards.back: 虽然她哭了，但是现在没事了。
cards.romanized: Suīrán tā kū le, dànshì xiànzài méishì le.
